cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question about garage sales. The passage provided gives some details, so I need to read through it carefully. First, the question is asking what we know about garage sales based on the text. The options are A, B, C, D. Let me look back at the passage. The relevant part starts with "Perhaps even more enjoyable than flea markets are the weekend outdoor garage sales which have now become very much a part of American culture." So it says they're part of American culture, which implies they're popular. That might relate to option C. Then it says, "These are held usually in the spring and fall..." So not only in spring, which rules out option A. The next part mentions that the homeowner is clearing out accumulated items. The text says "More than likely the goods on display are in good condition and great buys can be found..." So the goods are not useless (so B is incorrect). Also, it mentions that if the owner is selling the home, they may give things away below true worth, but that's a specific case, not all goods.
